gaps.title: Race condition in concurrent ticket counting
gaps.breaks: Double counting tickets when multiple processes run simultaneously
gaps.scenario: Two Celery workers both count tickets for the same tenant at the same time, resulting in 2x overage charges
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent Celery workers running the overage detection task simultaneously for the same tenant. Verify that ticket counts are not double counted and charges are only applied once.

gaps.title: Partial charge state without rollback
gaps.breaks: Overage detected and charged but notification fails, leaving customer unaware of charge
gaps.scenario: Paddle successfully charges $50 overage but email notification fails, causing support tickets and customer dissatisfaction
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a successful Paddle charge followed by a failed email notification. Implement a mechanism to detect and retry the notification, and verify that the system can recover from partial success states.

gaps.title: Tenant isolation in overage calculation
gaps.breaks: One tenant's ticket count affects another tenant's overage calculation
gaps.scenario: Tenant A with 3000 tickets and Tenant B with 1000 tickets both share the same company_id in the database, causing incorrect overage calculations
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies overage calculations are properly isolated by tenant. Create multiple tenants with overlapping company_ids and ensure each calculation is independent.

gaps.title: Idempotency failure in overage charging
gaps.breaks: Duplicate charges when the same overage event is processed multiple times
gaps.scenario: Paddle webhook for overage charge is received twice, causing the customer to be charged twice for the same overage
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates receiving the same overage charge event multiple times. Implement idempotency keys to ensure duplicate charges are not processed.

gaps.title: Silent failure in overage tracking
gaps.breaks: Overage charges applied but not recorded in database
gaps.scenario: Paddle charge succeeds but database write fails, creating a discrepancy between charges and tracking
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a successful Paddle charge followed by a database failure. Implement a reconciliation process to identify and fix mismatches between charges and tracking records.

gaps.title: State loss on restart during overage processing
gaps.breaks: In-progress overage calculation lost when service restarts
gaps.scenario: Celery worker crashes mid-calculation, losing partial results and causing some overages to be missed
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a service restart during overage calculation. Implement checkpointing or transaction-based processing to ensure calculations can resume after interruption.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 6

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Race condition in concurrent ticket counting
What breaks: Double counting tickets when multiple processes run simultaneously
Real scenario: Two Celery workers both count tickets for the same tenant at the same time, resulting in 2x overage charges
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent Celery workers running the overage detection task simultaneously for the same tenant. Verify that ticket counts are not double counted and charges are only applied once.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Partial charge state without rollback
What breaks: Overage detected and charged but notification fails, leaving customer unaware of charge
Real scenario: Paddle successfully charges $50 overage but email notification fails, causing support tickets and customer dissatisfaction
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a successful Paddle charge followed by a failed email notification. Implement a mechanism to detect and retry the notification, and verify that the system can recover from partial success states.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant isolation in overage calculation
What breaks: One tenant's ticket count affects another tenant's overage calculation
Real scenario: Tenant A with 3000 tickets and Tenant B with 1000 tickets both share the same company_id in the database, causing incorrect overage calculations
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies overage calculations are properly isolated by tenant. Create multiple tenants with overlapping company_ids and ensure each calculation is independent.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Idempotency failure in overage charging
What breaks: Duplicate charges when the same overage event is processed multiple times
Real scenario: Paddle webhook for overage charge is received twice, causing the customer to be charged twice for the same overage
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates receiving the same overage charge event multiple times. Implement idempotency keys to ensure duplicate charges are not processed.

GAP 5
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Silent failure in overage tracking
What breaks: Overage charges applied but not recorded in database
Real scenario: Paddle charge succeeds but database write fails, creating a discrepancy between charges and tracking
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a successful Paddle charge followed by a database failure. Implement a reconciliation process to identify and fix mismatches between charges and tracking records.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: State loss on restart during overage processing
What breaks: In-progress overage calculation lost when service restarts
Real scenario: Celery worker crashes mid-calculation, losing partial results and causing some overages to be missed
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a service restart during overage calculation. Implement checkpointing or transaction-based processing to ensure calculations can resume after interruption.
